narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD structural weakness because DXY CSM scores 20 on D1 and 30 on H4 with bearish fundamental bias confirmed by falling US 2Y yields and geopolitical easing removing safe-haven demand
2. GBP outperformance because GBP ranks strongest on both D1 CSM at 91 and H4 CSM at 100 with GBPUSD carrying aligned Buy signals on both timeframes
3. JPY weakness as funding currency because USDJPY JPY CSM sits at 61 and weakest among low-yielders while the entire JPY cross complex shows near-perfect internal correlation above 0.91

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPUSD leads the anti-USD move with D1 and H4 both aligned Buy while EURUSD lags with only H4 Buy and D1 Neutral, consistent with EUR being a relative underperformer against GBP despite EUR accelerating on H4 CSM. AUDUSD and NZDUSD are moving together as expected given their 0.94 correlation, but AUD CSM is fading relative to D1 strength at 79 versus H4 at 42, meaning NZD is carrying more of the commodity-currency leg. USDCHF Sell aligns with the broader USD weakness theme but CHF is fading hard on H4 CSM from 100 to 0, meaning CHF strength is a D1 holdover rather than a current driver.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed. VIX at 17 is elevated but rising only fractionally while S&P 500 hits 7230 at record highs and Gold at 4630 rising alongside equities signals dollar debasement rather than fear, a coherent risk-on with USD structural erosion as the unifying theme.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. CHF divergence: D1 CSM ranks CHF strongest at 100 but H4 CSM has collapsed to 0, meaning CHF safe-haven demand is unwinding intraday against its daily reputation
2. DXY printing up 0.13% on the day while every USD CSM score and fundamental signal is bearish, suggesting residual short covering rather than genuine USD bid

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: GBPUSD up - GBP strongest CSM both timeframes, USD weakest, aligned Buy signals - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: NZDUSD up - NZD H4 CSM at 96 leading AUD, H4 Buy signal intact, USD weak - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: USDCHF - CHF intraday collapse creates false signals against USD weakness theme
Watch: GBPUSD holding above H4 structure on next pullback confirms GBP leadership; a reversal below invalidates the entire anti-USD narrative

themes.theme: Energy sector mixed signals with crude price decline
Risk-on sentiment with equities posting record highs
Tech sector strength driving broad equity gains

key_observation: Strong risk-on sentiment with equity markets posting record highs and broad-based commodity strength, particularly in agricultural complex. Crude price decline offset by positive geopolitical developments on Iran negotiations. Tech strength via Nvidia and strong earnings backdrop support equity rally, which typically weighs on USD as safe-haven flows diminish.
watch: Continuation of equity momentum into next week, commodity price persistence, and follow-through on US-Iran negotiations. Monitor for any reversal in risk sentiment or fresh geopolitical headlines that could trigger flight-to-safety USD demand.
